title: linaria multicaulis subsp. aurasiaca
intro: Linaria multicaulis subsp. aurasiaca, also known as the Alpine Toadflax or Aurasiaca Toadflax, is a flowering plant species within the genus Linaria, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe respiratory issues and promote healing of wounds. The health benefits of Linaria multicaulis subsp. aurasiaca include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
